care.summary: An epiphytic cactus famous for its large, fragrant, nocturnal white flowers. Needs bright indirect light and more water than desert cacti.
care.light: Bright, indirect light. Morning sun is okay but avoid harsh afternoon sun.
care.water: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Keep slightly moist in spring/summer, reduce watering in winter.
care.soil: Well-draining, airy potting mix. Orchid bark mixed with standard potting soil works well.
care.humidity: Moderate to high humidity (50%+). Can benefit from misting.
care.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer every 2-4 weeks during the growing season.
tips: Blooms usually occur in late summer or early fall.
The flower opens at night and typically wilts by morning.
Needs a cool, dry winter resting period to set flower buds.
